title: Lutec - Budget Process Automation Discovery
summary: Discovery meeting between Daniel Cardona and Oscar Pinzón (Budget Director at Lutec) to explore automation opportunities in Lutec's construction budgeting process. Lutec is an electrical construction subcontractor with a 6-person team producing 10-12 budgets per week, where the main bottleneck is manually matching client-provided Excel activity lists against their internal APU (Unit Price Analysis) database of materials, labor, and suppliers. The proposed automation would use similarity matching to automatically suggest the closest APU codes for each client activity, allowing the team to focus on higher-value work like BIM analysis, anomaly detection, and cost projection rather than repetitive Excel lookups.

discussion_points.topic: Core Pain Point: Manual APU Matching Process
discussion_points.description: The budgeting team spends excessive time manually searching their internal APU (Unit Price Analysis) database to match client-requested construction activities. Each activity must be coded with the correct APU that contains the associated materials, labor, and supplier information. This manual matching process can take up to 3 days per budget and is highly repetitive.

Each construction client sends their own custom Excel spreadsheet with a quantity sheet ('sábana de cantidades'). Lutec cannot modify the client's file format. Different clients have different Excel structures and levels of specification detail, adding complexity to the matching process.

Lutec maintains an internal database of APUs organized by product families (tubing, cables, boxes, plates, etc.) with sub-categories for type, installation method (e.g., suspended), diameter, number of ducts, etc. Each APU contains the required materials/supplies, quantities per unit, labor costs, waste percentages, and supplier mappings.

The proposed solution would automatically analyze client Excel items and match them against Lutec's APU database using similarity analysis. The system would suggest the closest matching APU for each activity, and Oscar's team would only need to review, validate, and handle exceptions for unmatched items — drastically reducing manual lookup time.

Lutec has a dedicated BIM specialist who reviews 3D construction models to verify specifications, quantities, and identify anomalies (e.g., unknown installation heights for luminaires). BIM analysis is critical for accurate costing but is currently deprioritized because the team spends too much time on manual Excel work.

The full workflow includes: (1) receiving client Excel, (2) matching activities to APU codes, (3) associating materials/supplies/labor, (4) identifying missing products, (5) sourcing suppliers and base pricing, (6) passing supplier info to Purchasing for negotiation, (7) applying margin/profitability calculations, (8) returning the completed quotation in the client's original Excel format.

After quotation approval, Lutec generates a supply 'bag' (bolsa de insumos) for the project — a consolidated list of everything needed for the construction work. This becomes the cost control baseline, where deviations and incidents impact margin and profitability.
The budgeting department has 6 people working full-time, producing 10-12 budgets per week. One team member is dedicated exclusively to BIM analysis.

action_items.description: Send sample client Excel files (quantity sheets from different clients) to Daniel for analysis
Send the internal APU database with codes, materials, and supplier mappings
Send the APU template with formulas (material/labor separation, waste percentage calculations)
Analyze received files and propose an automation solution for APU matching

decisions: The primary automation target is the APU code matching process — automatically suggesting the closest matching APU for client-requested activities using similarity analysis
Oscar will send sample client Excel files and the internal APU database to Daniel for analysis
The quotation must always be returned in the client's original Excel format — no format modifications allowed
The automation should handle the lookup/matching while humans focus on review, BIM analysis, and exception handling
next_steps: Oscar sends sample client Excel files (from multiple clients) to Daniel
Oscar sends the internal APU database/codes with associated materials and supplier data
Oscar sends the APU template with formulas for material/labor separation and calculations
Daniel analyzes the data to design the automated matching solution
Follow-up meeting once Daniel has reviewed the files and has a proposed approach

diagrams.title: Current Manual Budget Workflow
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
    A["Client sends Excel\n(Quantity Sheet)"] --> B["Budget Team receives\ncustom Excel"]
    B --> C{"Activity recognized?"}
    C -->|Yes| D["Search APU Database\n(Manual Excel lookup)"]
    C -->|No| E["Review BIM models\nand construction plans"]
    E --> D
    D --> F["Copy-paste APU codes\ninto client Excel"]
    F --> G["Associate materials,\nlabor & supplies"]
    G --> H{"All items\navailable?"}
    H -->|Yes| I["Calculate margins\n& profitability"]
    H -->|No| J["Source new suppliers\n& get base pricing"]
    J --> K["Send to Purchasing\nfor negotiation"]
    K --> I
    I --> L["Return completed quotation\nin client Excel format"]
    L --> M["Generate supply bag\n(bolsa de insumos)"]
    M --> N["Cost control\n& project tracking"]

diagrams.title: Proposed Automated APU Matching System
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart LR
    A["Client Excel\nIngestion"] --> B["Activity Item\nExtractor"]
    B --> C["Similarity Matching\nEngine"]
    D[("APU Database\nMaterials, Labor,\nSuppliers")] --> C
    C --> E{"Match\nconfidence?"}
    E -->|High| F["Auto-assign APU code\nwith materials & labor"]
    E -->|Medium| G["Suggest top matches\nfor human review"]
    E -->|No match| H["Flag for manual\ncoding by team"]
    F --> I["Budget Review\nDashboard"]
    G --> I
    H --> I
    I --> J["Oscar reviews\n& validates"]
    J --> K["Generate quotation\nin client Excel format"]

diagrams.title: APU Data Structure
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
    A["APU Database"] --> B["Product Families"]
    B --> C["Tubing"]
    B --> D["Cables"]
    B --> E["Boxes"]
    B --> F["Plates"]
    B --> G["Other..."]
    C --> H["Type\n(EMT, PVC, etc.)"]
    H --> I["Installation Method\n(Suspended, Embedded, etc.)"]
    I --> J["Diameter\n(1/2, 3/4, 1 inch, etc.)"]
    J --> K["Number of Ducts\n(3, 4, 5, etc.)"]
    K --> L["APU Code"]
    L --> M["Materials list\nwith quantities"]
    L --> N["Labor requirements\nwith time estimates"]
    L --> O["Supplier mapping\nwith base prices"]
    L --> P["Waste percentage\ncalculations"]

diagrams.title: Client-to-Quotation Data Flow
diagrams.mermaid_code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client as Constructor Client
    participant Budget as Budget Team (Oscar)
    participant APU as APU Database
    participant BIM as BIM Specialist
    participant Purch as Purchasing Dept

    Client->>Budget: Send custom Excel (quantity sheet)
    Client->>BIM: Send construction plans & BIM models
    Budget->>APU: Search for matching APU codes
    APU-->>Budget: Return materials, labor & supplier data
    Budget->>BIM: Request verification of specifications
    BIM-->>Budget: Confirm specs, flag anomalies
    Budget->>Budget: Code all activities with APU references
    alt Missing products
        Budget->>Budget: Source alternative suppliers
        Budget->>Purch: Send supplier & base price info
        Purch-->>Budget: Confirm negotiated prices
    end
    Budget->>Budget: Apply margins & profitability formulas
    Budget->>Client: Return completed quotation (same Excel)
    Budget->>Budget: Generate supply bag for cost control
